Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add tool calling support via suspending SDK handlers

Tool calling bridge:
- ToolCallBridge converts Anthropic tool defs → SDK custom tools
- Handlers suspend (return unresolved Promise) when model invokes a tool
- tool_use content blocks streamed back in Anthropic SSE format
- Next request with tool_result resolves the handler, SDK resumes

SessionManager updates:
- Tracks tool bridges and pending tool call state per session
- Sessions with pending tool calls stay alive (not cleaned up)
- Tool result continuation routing via hasPendingToolCalls flag

Messages handler:
- Detects tool_result blocks in incoming messages
- Routes to pending session for continuation
- Supports text + tool_use mixed responses
- Streaming and non-streaming tool flows

Tests: 62 passing (11 new for tool-call-bridge)

fix(buffer): replace time.After polling with sync.Cond in AcquireForWriting

Replace inefficient 5ms polling loop in ring buffer acquisition with a
sync.Cond broadcast mechanism, eliminating unnecessary wake-ups and latency
under compositor back-pressure.

- Remove time.After-based polling in AcquireForWriting
- Add sync.Cond to Ring struct, signaled by MarkReleased
- Rename tryAcquireSlot to tryAcquireSlotLocked for clarity
- Update context cancellation handling with context.AfterFunc
- Add sync.Once to GPUBackend for one-time nil-atlas warnings
- Refactor internal/wayland/input tests for improved coverage
- Update README.md to correct demo binary count (22→18)
- Remove AUDIT.md (results incorporated into fixes)

This eliminates up to 5ms of latency per frame and reduces CPU overhead
during compositor back-pressure scenarios.

commit.message:
Fix edit buttons and availability results UX bugs (#145)

Fixes #140: Event edit button now navigates to edit page
Fixes #141: Availability request edit button now navigates to edit page
Fixes #143: Multiple dates can be expanded simultaneously in results heatmap
Fixes #144: Date expansion works independently of batch selection mode

Root cause for #140/#141: Edit route files were nested under the detail
layout routes (missing trailing underscore in Remix flat file convention).
The detail pages have no <Outlet/>, so child routes rendered invisibly.
Fix: Rename to $eventId_.edit.tsx and $requestId_.edit.tsx to opt out of
nesting, matching the existing pattern used by $requestId_.batch.tsx.

Root cause for #143: Single expandedDate state (string|null) only allowed
one date expanded at a time. Fix: Changed to expandedDates (Set<string>)
with a toggleExpanded callback.

Root cause for #144: The entire row click handler was either/or — batch
selection OR expansion, never both. Fix: Separated click targets with
stopPropagation — checkbox buttons handle selection, caret buttons handle

commit.message:
feat: add autonomous self-healing loop

SYNAPSE can now detect, diagnose, and fix its own bugs automatically.

Self-Healing Loop:
- Background thread checks /api/diagnostics every 5 minutes
- When errors exceed threshold (5+), sends error log to AI for diagnosis
- AI analyzes errors and generates search-replace code fixes
- Validates Python syntax before applying (compile() check)
- Only applies fixes with >70% confidence
- Creates git branch (synapse-heal-*), commits, pushes to GitHub
- Opens PR with diagnosis, confidence score, and files modified
- Cloud Build auto-deploys when PR is merged
- 30-minute cooldown between heal attempts to prevent loops

Safety Guards:
- search-replace only (no full file rewrites)
- Single occurrence match required
- Python syntax validation
- High confidence threshold (0.7)
- Cooldown prevents rapid-fire changes
- All fixes go through PR (human can review before merge)

New Endpoints:
- GET /api/healing — healer status, error count, action history
- POST /api/healing/trigger — manually trigger a heal check

UI: healing badge shows error count or healthy status

commit.message:
Add hold-time optimization and IR drop feedback to STA

Task 1: Hold-Time Optimization
- Add HoldFixResult struct to PostRouteOptimizer with metrics:
  num_violations_found, num_fixed, num_buffers_inserted, timing before/after
- Add public fix_hold_violations() method that iteratively:
  1. Runs STA to find hold-violating paths
  2. Inserts delay buffers via DRC-safe placement
  3. Re-checks timing to avoid creating setup violations
  4. Stops when all violations fixed or setup degrades

Task 2: IR Drop Feedback to STA
- Add apply_ir_drop_derating() to StaEngine accepting cell_name->voltage map
- Add set_ir_drop_alpha() and set_ir_drop_nominal_voltage() configuration
- Integrate voltage derating into effective_cell_derate() using alpha-power
  law: delay_scaled = delay_nominal * (V_nominal / V_actual)^alpha
- Default alpha=1.3 (MOSFET velocity saturation model)
- Zero-impact on existing code paths when no IR drop map is set

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add latch-based sequential timing and multi-supply-domain timing to STA engine

Latch timing support:
- LatchTimingInfo struct with opening/closing edge, borrow limit, borrowed time
- set_latch_timing_enabled() / set_latch_info() API for opt-in latch analysis
- find_latches() / compute_latch_edges() helpers
- Latch setup check: data_arrival + setup_time <= closing_edge
- Latch hold check: data_arrival >= opening_edge + hold_time
- Time borrowing: surplus slack at latch endpoint lent to downstream path
- DLATCH gates integrated into timing graph, forward/backward propagation,
  gate depth computation, and path extraction

Multi-supply-domain timing:
- VoltageDomain struct and set_voltage_domains() / set_cell_domain() API
- Voltage-dependent delay scaling: delay × (V_ref/V_domain)^alpha (alpha≈1.3)
- Level-shifter delay penalty when timing path crosses voltage domains
  (configurable via set_level_shifter_delay(), default 0.1ns)
- voltage_delay_scale() and level_shifter_penalty() helpers
- Scaling applied to both Liberty-based and default delay models

Both features are opt-in and do not affect existing timing analysis behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add SpatialIndex and negotiated congestion routing

- Add SpatialIndex class to physical.hpp/cpp: uniform-grid spatial hash
  supporting insert, remove, query (region + point), rebuild. Handles 1M+
  entries with O(sqrt(N)) query performance.

- Add standalone CongestionMap struct to global_router.hpp/cpp: tracks
  per-gcell usage, capacity, and historical congestion penalties with
  init, congestion_cost, update_history, is_overflowed, total_overflow.

- Add route_negotiated() to GlobalRouter: PathFinder-style iterative
  rip-up-and-reroute with increasing history penalties. Converges when
  overflow reaches zero or max iterations exhausted.

Add short-circuit power (Isc) modeling to power analysis engine

Implement Isc power calculation that models simultaneous PMOS/NMOS
conduction during input transitions. The model uses:

commit.message:
Fix Lagrangian relaxation convergence in tech mapper

Replace the broken lambda update rule (lambda += step * (0.5 - lambda)) which
always damped toward 0.5 regardless of area/delay balance, with a proper
subgradient descent:

- Derive total area and estimated delay from the cost decomposition
- Normalize violations against first-iteration reference values
- Update lambda via subgradient: lambda += step * (area_violation - delay_violation)
- Clamp lambda to [0.01, 0.99]
- Adaptive step size: halve when cost stalls for 2 consecutive iterations
- Early convergence exit when relative cost change < 1e-4 for 2 rounds
